[Question]
What could be the possible causes of toothache?

."'Answer
The possible causes of toothache include cavities, gum disease, tooth fracture, tooth sensitivity, tooth abscess, and jaw issues such as temporomandibular joint disorder (TMJ). Cavities occur when bacteria in the mouth produce acids that eat away at the tooth enamel. Gum disease is caused by plaque buildup leading to inflammation and infection of the gums. A tooth fracture can result from biting down on hard objects or experiencing trauma. Tooth sensitivity may be due to worn tooth enamel or exposed roots. An abscess forms when bacteria infect the inner pulp of the tooth, causing severe pain. Jaw issues like TMJ can also cause referred pain to the teeth.
